sentences.text: I'll just bring up, like, Once we get closer, we'll have, like, like, measurable metrics.
Yep, and like, yeah, setting, like, a cadence, that sort of thing.
Yeah, yep.
Okay, cool.
Good morning!
Hello?
Hello!
Hello!
How are y'all?
Good.
How you doing?
Good.
Living the dream.
Bob, good to see you again.
Rachel, it's so nice to meet you.
And then, we have Jessica, y'all's Customer Success Manager, on the call as well.
Hello!
Nice to meet you guys!
Nice to meet you.
How was y'all's weekend?
How was your week so far?
All good?
Rachel's had a more fun past couple of days than I.
She was on vacation.
Huh?
Say, where were you on vacation?
I was in Topsail Island, down in North Carolina.
Nice!
Yes.
The weather was beautiful, regardless of, … Or from that hurricane that was happening.
We had not a cloud in the sky.
In the past, that was, like, a really, like, that was a time to be there, was when that was passing by, but that's good that it was so pretty while you were there.
Yeah, my mom was like, are you evacuating?
Oh, yeah.
She was asking if we were evacuating, and I was like, what do you mean?
There's literally not a cloud, and I was like.
Nope.
Okay, that is interesting, because, like, they were sure making it seem like it was probably going to be, like, a problem, right?
Yes, yes, definitely, but at least where we were, we were perfectly safe, so that was good.
That is great.
That is so great.
Okay, well, good.
Well, lovely.
… So, let's see, I guess, Bob, I know that I inundated you with emails following our call last week.
Do you feel like I answered everything and got everything over to you that you needed?
Yes, thank you.
I just need to comb through… were you able to set me up with an account?
I might have missed an email for that.
So, no, that's where I dropped the ball, and we… I was just.
Oh, no worries, because I want to get Rachel in there, too.
Yes, so we'll make sure to get y'all up to speed with logins, that sort of thing.
But yes, I apologize on that.
But W9, you should have… Yep.
I did my darndest answering those reputation questions for you.
I don't know if you saw that, but….
Yep, yep.
Sounds like everything that you wanted to know, whether or not the dashboard could do, we can do, which is great.
But I know today, we are focused on just making sure that everything's up and running and, up to y'all's expectations on the Google Business Profile Automation front.
Rachel, I know… I know we have not chatted about the tool, so let me know if you have any questions.
At all.

But… Should we do, like, a… the two-cent tour of the system real quick for… just to bring Rachel up to speed, because I'm not sure if she knew what Mandy signed up for, or maybe not, just to bring her up.
Absolutely.
Okay.
Yeah.
I'll Venmo you the tour fee.
Here, let me just really quickly… I'm gonna run through… apologies, this is not one of your profiles, this is the first one that came to my noggin.
But yes, let me show you how this works really quick.
Okay… Jessica, do you care if I just give, like, a very wonky….
No, please.
Great, okay.
Okay, so, Jessica, yes, Mandy rolled out a… or sorry, not Jessica, Rachel.
Mandy rolled out our Google Business Profile automation tool.
So what it does is it integrates with your property management software to do 4 things on the profile daily.
We're gonna pull over the live pricing and availability of your floor plans.
We will highlight concessions and specials when they're available.
We also create daily posts, to really highlight the amenities that are offered at the property.
And then we lastly manage your Q&A feed.

So, the way that this looks on a profile… If we scroll down, Google kind of changes the order with this, so some profiles, the products first, and not the post, so on, so forth, but here are a look at those posts.

So, again, these are going to be photos that we pull over from your property management software, and then our AI tool really leans into the amenities that are being shown to make sure that we are using those amenity-rich keywords, so that when a prospective resident is searching, you know.
Apartments near me with a fitness center, you're more likely to come up in those searches.
Any questions on that?
Okay, this will also be where we post about, concessions and specials when they're running.

So we'll create a post about the concession or special once a week, and then once it expires, we will actually delete the post automatically, just to make sure that we're not referencing an outdated special.
For the products feed, so this is going to be the live pricing and availability of your floor plans.
If you click in, it's going to give a little bit more in-depth look of what the floor plan entails, and then we are ultimately driving website traffic back to your property website landing pages.
All right, and then a quick note on this.
I know that, Bob, we kind of spoke about it for a brief moment, but what we can also do here is, for any student properties, we can advertise it by the bed.
So we… we can talk more about that later, but just something, to make note of there.
And then lastly, this is going to be our Q&A feed.

So, what we did here is we looked at the most frequently asked questions to multifamily Google Business profiles, and we saw a lot of the times, the questions would be asked and maybe not ever answered, or answered not in the most timely manner.
So, to get ahead of that, what we've done is we place up to 20 questions on the profile And then we, edit the answer daily, even if it doesn't change.
Some of these questions are gonna be about, you know, rent specials.
Availability, those, you know, are more likely to change in regards to what the answer of the question is.

But even for something like office hours, what we're gonna do is we're still going to answer the question daily to, again, feed Google, you know, really relevant, frequent information to make sure we are capitalizing on the organic SEO that you're able to gain.
From optimizing your profile.
I know that was a very, very fast spiel.
Do you have any questions, Rachel?
Did that make sense?
Yes, so for the specials, does that pull from Entrada, from, like, our specials tab that we upload there?
Okay.
Exactly.
I need to make sure that those are on there, then.
And then… for the Q&A, if a question has already been asked, asks.
Jesus.
… Would that be… Like, would you guys repeat the question, or would you, … Would it just automatically notice that it's already been asked and answered, and that sort of thing?
So, if it's already been asked and answered, Jessica, we could just remove that question from the profile, right?
I believe so.
I can double check and confirm.
Okay.
But I believe so.
Yeah.
Yeah, because on some of our properties, like, we've already asked, like, frequently asked questions that are on our website, you know, like, pets, what kind of amenities, that sort of thing.
So, like, I wouldn't want, like, do you allow pets, and then do you allow pets, and then it just be, you know, multiple of the same question.
Yeah, absolutely.
We should be able to take those down, because we certainly, yeah, don't want redundant questions on the profile.
So yes.
Do you think… okay, yeah, that actually… that works, that's fine.
Okay.
Okay, perfect.

And then, let's see… I'm trying to think of anything else before we kind of share our deck, and then talk about, you know, what's a… Success looks like for y'all, brand guidelines, anything like that.
Do you have any other questions before we kind of hop in there?
That was all of mine.
Okay.
I don't know if Bob has any.
Doing good.
Okay, perfect.
Well then, Jessica, I will let you take it away.
Perfect.
Alright, well, gonna share my screen with you guys, put together a quick deck, that just has some things that we'll cover.

I'll go into the dashboard at the end of this and just kind of show you, like, where you'll be, what you can do in the dashboard, so you kind of have the whole rundown on that as well, too, and then I will, get you guys logins today, and share that with you so you can get logged in.
So… Couple things.
The first thing I always add in my decks is what your current contract is, in case you ever need to reference it, and you're like, what did… what do we have?
What do we need?
So I always put this in here.
I will share this with you at the end, so you can review it.
The first thing I always like to start with when I work with a new customer is understand what your measures of success are.
It's really important, as my title suggests, I'm your customer success manager, so success is kind of my thing.
So I want to just make sure that I'm aligning with your guys' business goals.
How you're measuring for those goals, and if there's any challenges you're facing now, maybe it's the reason why you signed up for Maven, you're like, we're trying to accomplish XYZ.
That's what I really want to understand at this first kind of kickoff.
So, I like to start in the first bucket, which is, what are your business goals?
They may be broad, and just like, hey, these are the things we're working on as a company, or they may be specific.
I just like to kind of note those down.
So, Rachel's been doing this a lot longer than I in this specific role for the company.
But….

we want to get leases, we want to get them as early as possible, and, you know, kind of build the brand, and I think that's… Google's a huge driver for that, specifically the business profile, you know, with SEO and search visibility.
What would you put in here, Rachel?

Yeah, I would 100% agree, like, I feel like the ultimate business goal is to get leases, and I think the measure of… for that success, specifically, would be, you know, our conversions, and how many actual, like, leads to leases we're receiving each month.
So, and I know that Bob and I would have discussed this, but yeah, just… and of course, completely understand, like, ultimately, your… company's goals to get leases.

Just one thing that I do want to note with our Google Business Profile Automation tool, while it absolutely, Bob, as you mentioned, like, makes sure that we standardize your brand, on Google and make sure that we reflect that, we definitely don't… say that this is a lead, like, a lead generating tool.

So, you know, what we're gonna do is, of course, make sure that we get that live pricing and availability of your floor plans, and, you know, ultimately, with time, we do see website traffic increase with this.
But yeah, as far as, you know, anything, like, measurable leads, like, lead conversions, lead-to-lease conversions, we don't recommend looking at this tool that way.
I just wanted to make that clear.
No, that's good framing.
I think, sort of, like, so those are our, like, broader business goals, like, specific.
Of course.
We'd be looking at things like, okay, how does optimizing our profile with this tool increase our impressions in search results.
What about, you know, click-throughs on the products, things like that?
I think the working theory would be that by using a tool like this.
Especially with optimizations to the frequently asked questions, regular postings, it would be those impressions and ultimately, like, traffic that we can see a lift there.
Absolutely, and that is one thing that I will be able to provide to you guys, is impression data.
So as we continue to work together, I'll be able to pull data for you, so what are your search impressions?
What are your website impressions?
How many people have clicked on the maps, within Google?

We'll be able to completely track those, see the differences, you know, increases, maybe is there an area, that's a little, you know, one property is maybe doing better than the Those are all things that I can help you guys kind of keep a measure on, and see how we're doing on that side.
Do you have… are you added to our Google My Business, or how does the connection work?
Like, can you see our historical, like, before, after, all that stuff?
Yes, so we can see, like, prior, for you, so we can kind of look at that year over year, things like that, we can compare to any dates, specifically.
Nice, cool.
Yeah.
So that's definitely something we'll be doing together.

… And then, I know Shelby mentioned it, and it's something that we'll go over, a little bit later, too, is, like, brand guidelines to make sure that, you know, everything is, like, consistent across the four properties, you know, kind of the styling, the branding, if each one is different, then we'll make sure that happens.
I'll go through all of that with you guys today as well, so if there are specific brand guidelines we want to make, we can do that as well.
Okay.

And then, when it comes to, like, I like to kind of understand, kind of, like, how your company operates, you know, when it comes to, like, the Google business profiles, prior to this, was anyone really managing those?
Was that just kind of something, like, is this new that you guys are putting, kind of, efforts into the Google side of things?
I'm gonna defer to Rachel, because I'm fresh here.
I know that she's had some stuff going, and my gut might be saying that this was, like, a test versus another tool, but I'm gonna mute myself here.

Yes, so, we typically post on our Google profiles for, all of our other properties that are not with Maven right now, that we post on them through Sochi, and then, prior to us, we were on Sprout for the last year, and then switched over to Sochi, and when we switched over to Sochi, there's a couple properties that just weren't doing well on social media, but we still wanted to have that constant, you know, the Google posts, and then the optimization, because we do that now through Sochi for other properties.
It was more so a supplemental, right?
Like, so that way we didn't have to be manually going in and doing each thing that we used to be able to do on, like, you know, on our Sochi account and Sprout accounts.
Yeah, absolutely.
And the four properties that we have with you guys, are those conventional, student, what does that breakdown look like?
They're kind of a combination, so we have, is it just… Sherwood lives southside, Chalfont, and Camelot.
Yes.
Okay, so we don't have Brittany on here.
Something we need to….
Actually, let me… Why did I feel like Brittany might have been on here?
Yeah, okay, sorry, I misspoke.
It is Brittany, Chalfont, Sherwood Towers, and Live Southside.
Okay, so Camelot's not on there, then.
And is Camelot… is that the Class A, Bob, that… there were two Class A properties that y'all were talking about?
Yeah, and so we don't have a Class A on there right now.
Camelot's real similar to, like, Chauffont-Sherwood location, and, like, that mix of student, kind of conventional.
Oh, got it, okay.
Okay, perfect.
Awesome.

Well, I think, you know, as we look over more data, I think we'll be able to kind of set more measures of success for you guys, and if there's ever anything that you're really focused on, of course, just… we'll always keep this updated.
Anytime we meet, I'll bring this slide back up, I'll fill it in after our call today.
But our… the last one I guess, I didn't touch on, is there any challenges that you guys are facing, that you are hoping that we can overcome together?
Like, is there anything that sticks out to you guys of, like, this has been a really big challenge for us, that maybe these four properties.
Or just overall.
I can't think of anything, can you?
I think there's pressure for… from all of our properties with, like, new units coming on, with more amenities.
Review… review garnering was one we talked about, and I know you guys have that supplemental.
You know, so that's different than this particular product.
But yeah, I think just increased competitive pressure and making the profile stand out is what I could frame it as the biggest challenge.
Yeah, absolutely.
And then, are you guys in the same room?
I'm sorry, I just saw you….
I'm like, yeah, do we have anything else going on?
Yeah.
Boom!
But my… this mic is, like, not sensitive enough where she can talk and it pick up.
Same with her, so it's….
I just wanted to make sure I wasn't crazy, because I was like, I swear he looked at her, but maybe he was just, you know, looking around.
Oh, there, there he is, behind her.
Perfect.
Well, thank you guys for that.
Again, anything we ever want to add to this, or if we shift, I know we're halfway through the year, which is insane to say, I know things like that can come up.
Goal setting, if you guys ever do that, would love to be a part of that conversation and make sure that I can help you meet those.
Yeah, thank you.
I have not put, … anything together yet as far as how those profiles are trending, like, their performance and that, so anything you can gather would be huge.
And, I'm embarrassed to say I don't… see the contract in our thread.
I'm looking for it.
Oh, I mean, that could have definitely been….
It might be in Mandy's inbox still.
I want to add it to our… we've got a contract file just for all our properties, I gotta toss it in there.
I believe… let me see if I… Billing questions… okay, I think it's right here.
I'm.
in there, isn't it?
Okay.
But I… no, no, I got you.
I mean, Bob, I really did, like… I spammed you last.
So….
Do you know what the file name is?
I can… here it is, I've got it.
I think I see it.
Okay.
Well, I just sent it over via email, so apologies in advance here.
No, you're good.
Thank you for that.
Yeah, of course.
Maybe we'll start tracking how many emails you get are from Shelby at some point.
We'll track it as well, too.
Alright, put that in the KPI, yep.
Yeah, we'll see.
And we'll want to make sure that one's high, Shelby, so you should start crafting some emails early.
Perfect.
Well, next thing I just want to go over, and we already kind of hit on this, is metrics.
So that will be something that we'll be able, to go over.
What I like to do is, early on in our relationship, have a 30, 60, 90 day check-in, just to see how things are going as the product is doing its thing, just to kind of check in.
And then about that 90-day mark, we can do, like, a quarter-over-quarter review of, like, how things have been going for the last 3 months, what are our impressions looking like.
For those four properties, and just see, is there anything we want to change, update, especially when it comes to, like, brand guidelines, once we've seen it kind of working, for 90 days.
I always like to just add this in as, like, kind of what Shelby already touched on, is, like, what Maven is actually doing, which is going to be optimizing that profile.

I won't spend too much time on this since she covered it at the beginning of the call, but that is really what our focus is here, is optimizing, your Google business profile, making sure, you're kind of top in the ranks there when it comes, to visibility, and impressions.
And then just, like, taking kind of that weight off you guys, because this is all gonna run for you on the back end.
There's going to be ways for you guys to check in on it if there's things you want to do, but it's really gonna do all the work for you, which is the nicest part about all of this.

Which is, I like to add this in just to kind of show, you know, like, how much time you're saving, rather than, Rachel, if this would be you doing this, or if this is a property manager having to do, kind of this work, just kind of like the breakdown.
So you're looking at about, annually, 22 days for one property to do the things that Maven's doing.

For 4 properties, that'd be 88 days across the year that are spent Optimizing the profile, and kind of doing… I don't like to say mundane, but they're… they're not fun, always, to try to think of something to post, so the system's doing it for you.
You don't have to come up… you know, previous to working as a customer success manager, I was a property manager, and I can promise you, I used to sit around and be like, what should I post today?
And AI didn't exist then.
I couldn't even ask ChatGPT.
So I think that there's a lot of opportunity here, and if you find more properties.
that you think would benefit from this time and cost savings, that Maven kind of drives, of course, you know who to let know, because Shelby will be in your inbox, increasing that KPI.
One thing, and before we go to this, I want to jump into the dashboard to show you guys what you guys can look at, and kind of what everything's doing.
I'll also show you some of your live profiles, just so you can kind of see, like, what already has been done.
So I'm gonna pull that up quickly, and we'll also look at brand guidelines.
And we'll be… we'll be sure to get y'all logins for this, following this call as well.
Yes, absolutely.
Alright, so… This is what we're looking at, can you guys see my screen?
I mean, you can see it.
Perfect.
So, we're logged in.

The first thing I always like to point out, it is… the dashboard is referred to as Rentable, on this side, so just keeping that in mind, Rentable is kind of like our umbrella name, and then Maven's the product.
There's a couple different things that can be done in this dashboard.
You guys are going to be mostly focused on this Google Business, tab.
Everything kind of above this is really related to our lead generation product, which is called Rentable.
So you can avoid these, sections and just focus on the Google Business section.
So when you go in here, first thing you'll see, those are the four properties that we're using, for the Google Business proper, product.
First thing is upcoming posts.
I like to start here, because it's kind of where you're going to be spending the most time.
This is going to be showing you what is upcoming.
We… we have about, I think, 13 posts, or is that 2 weeks?
Is that 2 weeks?
Yeah, that's 2 weeks in advance that post, you can kind of see what's going on.
And when things are approved.
So we kind of have a system on our side of, like, you know, sometimes we double-check the AI to make sure they're not saying anything, like, wonky.
We don't want anything crazy going on.
So you'll see that those are what we have two weeks in advance approved.
If you ever see anything, if you're looking through this, and you're like, I don't like that word, or I wish it said it a little bit different, you do have the opportunity to edit upcoming posts.

Or if you're just straight up, like, I don't like this, and I don't want this posted, or maybe it's just a bad time for something like that to be posted, if something, you know, if there's a pool at one of your properties and it just broke that week, maybe we don't want to post about the pool, and you're like, let's deny the pool post for this week.
So you can do that.
When you deny a post, it is going to regenerate a post for that day, so another one will come up, but you do have a lot of opportunity to go in here and change some things up.
You can also then see previous posts.
So if you want to see what has been posted previously without actually going to the Google profile, you can do it in here.
So you choose the one, you do view, and you'll see… what it's gonna show you is the text that was put out, and then if you hit View here, this'll kick you directly to the profile.
So if you're like, I want to see that, what that looked like live on the profile, it'll kick you right over to it.
One thing that I did notice, is… I think for a couple… I checked a couple of them.
It looks like we have about 12 photos each.

I always say, the more photos, the better, so if you ever do have, you know, if someone goes and takes photos of the property and you want to get those put in, you either need to put them into your property management software Entrada, or if you have ones that maybe you don't necessarily want to upload into Entrada, because I'm not always sure how that translates on your side, if you have, let's say, like, stock photos of, like.

Like, someone sitting at a computer, or someone walking a dog, send those to me, and we can have them uploaded on our side for that profile, if we want to highlight some, like, specific things about the communities.

And I'll send over, as well, after this, kind of just, like, the best, like, you know, styles of the photos, sizing, all of that, like, kind of, like, best practices when it comes to those things, so you have them in case you ever want to do, add a couple things like that.
Next thing under previous posts is going to be your Q&A, so you can see what questions we've already preloaded into here, for each property.

I know, Rachel, you mentioned, like, we don't always want to duplicate questions, so if there's any ones in here that you want to change or anything like that, just let me know, but these are the ones that were pre, like, loaded in during the implementation process.
And then, for products, right now, it looks like we just have your floor plans in there, for what's available.
One thing that we can also add to here, and if this is in your Entrato, we can add it in, is your amenities.
So if you have photos and special amenities you want to highlight, we can, connect those to the feed, and they will also populate on your products.
So if we click back over, to the profile here, we see the floor plans available here.
If we added, the amenities, we'd see, like, community amenities.

Do you have a pool, maybe a dog park, if you have those in Entrada, as well as in-unit amenities, maybe a washer-dryer, a balcony with a lake view, anything like that, if you want to highlight those, we can definitely get an amenities feed set up for each of those.
And that's just gonna help you stand out even further on your Google Business profile, especially if someone's looking for a specific amenity, like a pet park or a pet wash, things like that.
Alright, so that's really the main things we'll focus on here, for you guys.
Again, there's a lot of tabs here, but I usually just say, since you guys are just using our Google Business Profile product right now, focus on this.
If you ever have a question, like, what is social postings, or what is the review section?
We would be happy to go through those different kind of add-ons to the product, like our social AI product, reputation, things like that.
The last thing that I'll focus on, and this is something that we will update on our end, you just have to tell us what you want is your brand guidelines.
So, we know either you just have, like, a total, like, company guidelines of, like, these are the words we like to use, or these are the words we don't like to use.
Or if you have hashtags, things like that, we can add those into the system and make sure that we're kind of fitting the vibe of each, either the company or each property individually.
So we can do either one.
If you want to have, like, a really formal tone for one of your properties, we can set that up.
If you have, like, a casual tone for another, we can set that up.
If there's words you don't like to be used, I know sometimes, like, people don't like to use the word, like, tenant.
We'd rather use the word, like, resident.
Just let us know, and we can set those up.
That way, those are kind of filtered out of the pre-populated post, so we're not using those wordings.

If there's something you call an amenity, like something very special, other than it's not just a sauna, it's something else, a resort-style sauna, let us know all those things, and we'll make sure we kind of tailor those in there, so they're done for you guys.
Nice.
Any questions, or any brand guidelines you know, like, off the bat that you guys might wanna put in?
My head's already thinking of thoughts.
Do we do anything right now, Rachel, for the, … like, Sochi, is there, like, a brand guideline kind of doc that it references?
So our AI tool with Sochi has, like, pre… for, like, across all platforms, so something similar, like.

… the… referring as residents instead of tenants and that sort of thing, so we have that set up for all those other properties, as far as anyone… all of these properties that are not on Sochi, … Obviously, we don't have them set up with any of those guidelines, but we could probably use the same, like, the similar ones that we use there.
So it's the same across all properties.
Yeah, absolutely, feel free.
You can send over the document that you already have, or if you want to break it down or remove things, and then I can send it over to the team and get things put in.
If there's anything that maybe we have questions about or aren't specific on our side, we can definitely let you know if there's anything like that.
Okay.

And then, I did just want to show what the amenities feed would look like if you added it, so this is just an example of another property that has its amenities feed put in there, of, like, secured entry is put in there, really anything that you want to highlight, we can get those added.
Again, they just need to be through your feed in Entrada, and we can bring them in.
Any questions?
Perfect.
Okay, awesome.
So I will get you guys logins today.
I will send that over to you guys so you can get logged in.
Play around.
If you log in and you look at things, you have questions, definitely let me know.
I'm happy to go over this again, … or if there's anyone else you think should be added, like a part of your team, that you'd want to have access to this, just let me know.
Like I said, I like to do a 30, 60, 90-day check-in, for, like, our first 3 months together, and then after that, kind of decide what cadence works best for us.
Do we want to do, like, every other month, once a quarter, whatever works.
… I like to just have, like, a really close contact with you guys.
That way, if there's anything you ever need, you know how to get ahold of me, and we have a place to kind of talk about those things.
So, looking 30 days from now, if we want to look at the calendar, if that works for you guys…
We are looking at… Maybe, if my calendar will load, sorry.
30 days.
Thank you, calendar.
We're looking at end of September, so that's, like, the week of September 22nd, or the last week of the 29th.
What stands out to you guys?
Any, any vacations coming up?
I know, Rachel, you just got back from one.
Bob, are you headed on vacation anytime soon?
Here, I can… are you… are you good either of those weeks?
Yeah.
Bob got kicked out a little bit.
Everything's good for me.
Yeah, so maybe… I would say Tuesdays… Tuesday the 23rd, Probably would work.
Let me just pull up our… schedule is one… one moment.
